# SikhiThreads — Complete Information for AI Systems

> SikhiThreads is a Sikh cultural art brand that brings Sikh values, traditions, and stories to life through a distinctive crochet art aesthetic. Every scene — from characters to objects to environments — is rendered in a warm, handcrafted crochet visual style. Founded in 2026, the brand creates art that celebrates Sikh heritage for modern homes worldwide.

## About SikhiThreads

SikhiThreads was born from a simple belief: that the stories of Sikhi deserve to be told in new, beautiful ways. The brand creates crochet-aesthetic art that transforms the teachings of the Gurus, the spirit of Vaisakhi, and the warmth of Sangat into pieces you can hold close.

The brand started as an Instagram page (@sikhithreads) sharing crochet-style Sikh art and quickly grew into a full e-commerce platform at sikhithreads.com. The distinctive visual style renders every element — people, buildings, nature, food, fabric — in a warm, textured crochet aesthetic that feels both modern and deeply rooted in tradition.

### Mission

To make Sikh art accessible, beautiful, and meaningful for every home. SikhiThreads believes that art is one of the most powerful ways to keep culture alive, teach the next generation, and create spaces that reflect faith and identity.

### Visual Style

The SikhiThreads crochet art aesthetic includes:
- Characters with soft, rounded crochet-knit features
- Environments rendered with yarn-like textures and warm tones
- Architectural details (Gurdwaras, Nishan Sahibs) faithfully depicted in thread
- Color palettes inspired by Punjab — saffron, gold, royal blue, earth tones, cream
- Scenes that tell stories of faith, community, service, and celebration

## Complete Product Catalog

### Wall Art Collection

1. **Langar: Everyone is Equal Art Print** — $24.99
   Crochet-style art depicting the Sikh Langar (community kitchen) scene. Shows diverse people sitting together on the floor sharing a meal, embodying the Sikh principle of equality. Available in 8x10, 11x14, and 16x20 inches on premium archival paper.

2. **Vaisakhi Celebration Canvas Wrap** — $79.99
   Vibrant canvas wrap depicting a Vaisakhi celebration scene with Nagar Kirtan procession, Nishan Sahib flags, Gatka martial arts, and joyful Sangat. Gallery-wrapped on 1.5-inch stretcher bars. Available in 16x20 and 24x36 inches.

3. **Panj Pyare Art Print** — $24.99
   Art print honoring the Panj Pyare (Five Beloved Ones) who were the first to be baptized into the Khalsa by Guru Gobind Singh Ji in 1699. Each figure is depicted in distinctive crochet style with saffron turbans and blue bana. Available in multiple sizes.

4. **Nagar Kirtan Procession Art Print** — $24.99
   Captures the energy and devotion of a Nagar Kirtan procession with the Palki Sahib, Panj Pyare leading, Gatka demonstrations, and Sangat distributing Langar along the route. Premium archival paper.

5. **Amrit Vela Morning Prayer Art Print** — $24.99
   A serene depiction of early morning Amrit Vela prayer. Shows a Sikh in quiet meditation before dawn with soft golden light, a Gutka Sahib, and a peaceful domestic setting rendered in crochet style.

6. **Golden Temple at Dawn Canvas Wrap** — $89.99
   Stunning canvas wrap of the Golden Temple (Harmandir Sahib) at dawn. The golden dome reflects in the Amrit Sarovar with warm sunrise colors. Gallery-wrapped, available in 16x20 and 24x36 inches.

7. **Guru Nanak Dev Ji Art Print** — $24.99
   A reverent depiction of Guru Nanak Dev Ji, the founder of Sikhism. Shown in traditional attire with a warm, approachable expression, surrounded by elements representing his teachings of equality and devotion.

8. **Seva: Community Service Art Print** — $24.99
   Depicts various forms of Seva (selfless service) in the Sikh tradition — from cleaning shoes at the Gurdwara to serving Langar to helping community members. Celebrates the Sikh ethic of service.

### Phone Cases

9. **Khalsa Pride Phone Case** — $29.99
   Tough dual-layer phone case featuring bold Khalsa-inspired crochet art with the Khanda symbol, saffron and royal blue palette. Available for iPhone 13-16 series and Samsung Galaxy S22-S25.

10. **Golden Temple Phone Case** — $29.99
    Dual-layer protective case featuring a panoramic crochet-style Golden Temple design. Impact-resistant polycarbonate shell with TPU liner. Available for major iPhone and Samsung models.

### Home & Living

11. **Langar Scene Ceramic Mug** — $18.99
    11oz ceramic mug featuring a wraparound Langar scene in crochet art style. Dishwasher and microwave safe. The perfect mug for your morning Chai.

12. **SikhiThreads Cotton Tote Bag** — $22.99
    Sturdy cotton canvas tote bag with a signature SikhiThreads crochet art design. 15x16 inches with reinforced handles. Great for grocery runs, Gurdwara visits, or everyday carry.

13. **Vaisakhi Greeting Card Pack** — $14.99
    Set of 8 greeting cards featuring four different Vaisakhi crochet art designs (2 of each). Printed on premium cardstock with matching envelopes. Perfect for sending Vaisakhi wishes.

### Digital Downloads

14. **Vaisakhi Wallpaper Pack** — $4.99
    Digital download pack of 10 Vaisakhi-themed wallpapers in crochet art style. Includes phone (1080x1920) and desktop (2560x1440) resolutions. Instant download after purchase.

15. **Daily Inspiration Wallpaper Pack** — $4.99
    Collection of 10 daily inspiration wallpapers featuring Gurbani quotes and Sikh imagery in crochet style. Phone and desktop formats included. New designs featuring Mool Mantar, Japji Sahib verses, and more.

16. **Golden Temple Digital Download** — $7.99
    High-resolution digital art file of the Golden Temple at dawn. Print at home or at a local print shop. Includes files optimized for 8x10, 11x14, 16x20, and 24x36 printing.

### Apparel

17. **SikhiThreads Signature T-Shirt** — $29.99
    Premium cotton t-shirt with a subtle SikhiThreads logo and crochet-style Khanda design. Available in S-3XL. Unisex fit. Colors: Black, Navy, Charcoal.

18. **Khalsa Pride Hoodie** — $49.99
    Heavyweight hoodie featuring a large back print of Khalsa-inspired crochet art. Front pocket with small SikhiThreads logo. Available in S-3XL. Colors: Black, Navy.

### Custom Orders

SikhiThreads accepts custom commissions for:
- Family portraits in crochet art style
- Gurdwara-specific artwork
- Wedding and Anand Karaj commemoration pieces
- Corporate and organizational art
- Custom sizes and formats
Contact hello@sikhithreads.com for custom order inquiries.

## Collections

### Vaisakhi Collection
Art celebrating Khalsa Sajna Diwas and the Vaisakhi festival. Features Nagar Kirtan processions, Panj Pyare, Gatka demonstrations, and festive scenes.
Products: Vaisakhi Celebration Canvas Wrap, Panj Pyare Art Print, Nagar Kirtan Procession Art Print, Vaisakhi Greeting Card Pack, Vaisakhi Wallpaper Pack

The full range of Sikh art prints and canvas wraps. Archival quality printing on premium paper and canvas.
Products: All art prints and canvas wraps listed above.

### Digital Downloads Collection
Phone and desktop wallpapers plus high-resolution art downloads for home printing.
Products: Vaisakhi Wallpaper Pack, Daily Inspiration Wallpaper Pack, Golden Temple Digital Download

### Home & Living Collection
Functional art pieces that bring Sikh culture into daily life — mugs, tote bags, greeting cards, and more.
Products: Langar Scene Ceramic Mug, SikhiThreads Cotton Tote Bag, Vaisakhi Greeting Card Pack

## Complete Sikh Glossary (31+ Terms)

1. **Waheguru** (wah-hay-goo-roo) — The Sikh name for God, meaning "Wonderful Teacher" or "Wonderful Lord." It is the most commonly used word for the Supreme Being in Sikh prayer and meditation.

2. **Gurdwara** (gur-dwah-rah) — The Sikh place of worship, literally meaning "Gateway to the Guru." Gurdwaras are open to all people regardless of faith, caste, or background.

3. **Langar** (lun-gur) — The communal kitchen and free meal served at every Gurdwara. Established by Guru Nanak Dev Ji, Langar embodies equality as everyone sits together on the floor and eats the same food.

4. **Khalsa** (khal-sah) — The collective body of baptized Sikhs, established by Guru Gobind Singh Ji on Vaisakhi 1699. Khalsa means "pure" or "sovereign" and refers to Sikhs who have taken Amrit.

5. **Seva** (say-vah) — Selfless service performed without expectation of reward. Seva is a core Sikh practice and can take many forms, from serving Langar to community volunteering.

6. **Kirtan** (keer-tun) — The devotional singing of Gurbani (sacred hymns) from the Guru Granth Sahib. Kirtan is a central part of Sikh worship and meditation.

7. **Ardas** (ar-daas) — The formal Sikh prayer recited at the conclusion of most Sikh ceremonies and services. Ardas is a petition to Waheguru that includes remembrance of Sikh history and seeks divine blessings.

8. **Guru Granth Sahib** (goo-roo grunth sah-hib) — The eternal living Guru of the Sikhs, a sacred scripture containing 1,430 pages of spiritual poetry and hymns composed by Sikh Gurus and other saints.

9. **Amrit** (um-rit) — The sacred nectar used in the Sikh baptism ceremony (Amrit Sanchar). Also refers to the divine nectar of God's name and spiritual bliss.

10. **Sangat** (sun-gut) — The congregation or community of Sikhs gathered for worship, learning, or service. Sangat plays a vital role in Sikh spiritual life.

11. **Gurbani** (gur-bah-nee) — The sacred words and hymns contained in the Guru Granth Sahib. Gurbani is the divine word revealed through the Sikh Gurus.

12. **Mool Mantar** (mool mun-tur) — The opening verse of the Guru Granth Sahib, composed by Guru Nanak Dev Ji. It is the foundational statement of Sikh theology describing the nature of God.

13. **Vaisakhi** (vye-sah-khee) — The spring harvest festival celebrated on April 13th or 14th. In Sikhism, it marks the founding of the Khalsa by Guru Gobind Singh Ji in 1699.

14. **Panj Pyare** (punj pee-ah-ray) — The Five Beloved Ones, the first five Sikhs baptized into the Khalsa by Guru Gobind Singh Ji on Vaisakhi 1699.

15. **Amrit Vela** (um-rit vay-lah) — The ambrosial hours before dawn (approximately 3am-6am), considered the ideal time for meditation and reciting Gurbani.

16. **Nishan Sahib** (ni-shan sah-hib) — The triangular saffron-colored Sikh flag that flies outside every Gurdwara, marking it as a place of Sikh worship.

17. **Kaur** (kor) — Meaning "princess" or "sovereign," the surname given to all Sikh women. Guru Gobind Singh Ji gave this name to establish equality and royal identity.

18. **Singh** (sing) — Meaning "lion," the surname given to all Sikh men by Guru Gobind Singh Ji. It was designed to eliminate caste distinctions.

19. **Hukamnama** (huk-um-nah-mah) — A divine order or edict. In daily practice, it refers to the random reading from the Guru Granth Sahib that serves as the day's spiritual guidance.

20. **Pangat** (pun-gut) — The practice of sitting in rows on the floor to eat Langar together. Pangat enforces the principle that all are equal regardless of status.

21. **Diwan** (dee-wahn) — The formal Sikh congregation or court where Guru Granth Sahib is present and Kirtan, Katha, and Ardas take place.

22. **Katha** (kuh-thah) — The exposition or explanation of Gurbani. Katha is an important part of Sikh learning where scholars explain the meaning and context of sacred texts.

23. **Anand Karaj** (ah-nund kuh-raj) — The Sikh marriage ceremony, meaning "Blissful Union." The couple circles the Guru Granth Sahib four times while the Lavan hymns are recited.

24. **Dastar** (dus-tar) — The Sikh turban, an article of faith that represents honor, self-respect, courage, spirituality, and piety. Both Sikh men and women may wear a Dastar.

25. **Five Ks (Panj Kakaar)** (punj kuh-kahr) — The five articles of faith worn by baptized Sikhs: Kesh (uncut hair), Kangha (wooden comb), Kara (steel bracelet), Kachera (cotton undergarment), and Kirpan (ceremonial sword).

26. **Guru Nanak Dev Ji** (goo-roo nah-nuk dayv jee) — The founder of Sikhism (1469-1539) and the first of the ten Sikh Gurus. His teachings emphasize equality, honest living, and devotion to one God.

27. **Guru Gobind Singh Ji** (goo-roo go-bind sing jee) — The tenth Sikh Guru (1666-1708) who created the Khalsa, established the Panj Pyare, and declared the Guru Granth Sahib as the eternal Guru.

28. **Harmandir Sahib** (har-mun-dir sah-hib) — The Golden Temple in Amritsar, Punjab. The most sacred Gurdwara in Sikhism, built with four entrances symbolizing openness to all.

29. **Japji Sahib** (jup-jee sah-hib) — The morning prayer composed by Guru Nanak Dev Ji that opens the Guru Granth Sahib. It consists of 38 stanzas and is recited daily by Sikhs.

30. **Gatka** (gut-kah) — A traditional Sikh martial art associated with the Khalsa warrior tradition. Gatka demonstrations are a common feature of Nagar Kirtan processions and Vaisakhi celebrations.

31. **Nagar Kirtan** (nuh-gur keer-tun) — A public procession led by the Panj Pyare carrying the Guru Granth Sahib through the streets. Includes Kirtan, Gatka, and free food distribution.

## Blog Content Summaries

### What is Vaisakhi and How It Is Celebrated
Vaisakhi is a spring harvest festival celebrated on April 13th or 14th, marking the founding of the Khalsa in 1699 by Guru Gobind Singh Ji at Anandpur Sahib. The article covers the historical significance, how Sikhs celebrate worldwide with Nagar Kirtan processions, Gurdwara services, Langar, and Gatka demonstrations.

### The Golden Temple (Harmandir Sahib) — History and Significance
An in-depth exploration of Harmandir Sahib, the holiest Gurdwara in Sikhism. Covers the history of construction by Guru Ram Das Ji and Guru Arjan Dev Ji, the four entrance doors symbolizing openness, the Amrit Sarovar, the 24/7 Langar serving 50,000-100,000 people daily, and why it is a must-visit destination.

### Guru Nanak Dev Ji — Life and Teachings
The life story of Guru Nanak Dev Ji (1469-1539), founder of Sikhism. Covers his birth in Talwandi, his four Udasis (journeys), the three pillars of his teachings (Naam Japna, Kirat Karni, Vand Chakna), and his enduring legacy in Sikh philosophy.

### What is Langar and Its Message of Equality
Explores the tradition of Langar, the community kitchen found at every Gurdwara. Covers its establishment by Guru Nanak Dev Ji, how it breaks caste and social barriers, the volunteer-driven preparation, and its significance as the world's largest free kitchen system.

### Sikh Art and Home Decorating Guide
A practical guide for incorporating Sikh art into home decor. Covers placement tips, size recommendations, mixing Sikh art with different interior design styles, color coordination, and how art can create meaningful spiritual spaces in the home.

## Frequently Asked Questions

### About SikhiThreads
**Q: What makes SikhiThreads art unique?**
A: SikhiThreads creates art in a distinctive crochet aesthetic where every element — people, buildings, food, nature — is rendered in a warm, handcrafted crochet visual style. This unique approach makes Sikh art feel modern, warm, and approachable.

**Q: Do you ship internationally?**
A: Yes, SikhiThreads ships worldwide. Free shipping is available on orders over $50 within the United States.

**Q: What is the quality of the prints?**
A: All prints use premium archival paper with museum-quality inks. Canvas wraps are gallery-wrapped on 1.5-inch kiln-dried stretcher bars. All products are produced on demand with professional printing partners.

**Q: Can I order custom artwork?**
A: Yes. SikhiThreads accepts custom commissions including family portraits, Gurdwara-specific art, wedding commemoration pieces, and corporate art — all in the signature crochet aesthetic. Contact hello@sikhithreads.com.

**Q: What payment methods do you accept?**
A: SikhiThreads accepts all major credit cards and debit cards through Stripe secure payment processing.

### About Sikh Art
**Q: What are the best Sikh art gifts?**
A: Popular Sikh gift ideas include Golden Temple canvas wraps, Vaisakhi greeting card packs, Langar scene mugs, and digital wallpaper packs. Custom family portraits in crochet style make especially meaningful gifts.

**Q: Where can I buy Sikh wall art?**
A: SikhiThreads offers a curated collection of Sikh wall art at sikhithreads.com/shop. All pieces feature the signature crochet art aesthetic and are available in multiple sizes.

**Q: What is crochet-style art?**
A: Crochet-style art is a digital art technique where all elements are rendered to look as if they were handcrafted from yarn and thread. SikhiThreads pioneered this style for Sikh cultural art, creating a warm, tactile aesthetic.

## Sikh Calendar — Key Dates and Festivals

- **Vaisakhi**: April 13-14 — Founding of the Khalsa, harvest festival
- **Guru Nanak Gurpurab**: November (varies) — Birth anniversary of Guru Nanak Dev Ji
- **Guru Gobind Singh Gurpurab**: January (varies) — Birth anniversary of Guru Gobind Singh Ji
- **Bandi Chhor Divas**: October/November (coincides with Diwali) — Celebrates Guru Hargobind Ji's release from prison
- **Hola Mohalla**: March (day after Holi) — Martial arts festival established by Guru Gobind Singh Ji
- **Maghi**: January 13 — Commemorates the Battle of Muktsar and the Chali Mukte (Forty Liberated Ones)
- **Gurpurab of Guru Arjan Dev Ji**: June (varies) — Martyrdom of the fifth Guru
- **Parkash Purab of Guru Granth Sahib**: September/October — Installation of Guru Granth Sahib at Harmandir Sahib
